target.title: feat(provider): add XCP-ng provider
target.body:
## Summary

This PR adds a full XCP-ng provider to Crabbox and wires it through the CLI, config model, provider registry, docs, and test suite.

At a high level, the new provider is a direct SSH-lease backend for Linux guests on a self-hosted XCP-ng pool. Crabbox now knows how to authenticate to XAPI, resolve template and storage placement, clone and label a VM, generate and attach a per-lease config-drive, wait for XCP-ng guest metrics to report a usable IPv4 address, bootstrap the guest over SSH, and then use the normal Crabbox sync/run/release flows against that lease.

## What This PR Adds

### New provider implementation

- Adds `internal/providers/xcpng` as a new provider package with provider registration, backend lifecycle handling, Xen API client logic, cloud-init/config-drive generation, typed provider state, and focused provider tests.
- Registers `xcp-ng` as a direct-only provider with `ssh`, `crabbox-sync`, and `cleanup` capabilities on `target=linux`.
- Keeps XCP-ng outside coordinator-managed capacity: the CLI talks directly to the XAPI endpoint and provisions leases itself.

### Provisioning and lease lifecycle

- Implements XCP-ng acquisition flow in `internal/providers/xcpng/backend.go`:
  - validate provider configuration;
  - open an XAPI session;
  - list existing Crabbox-managed VMs for slug allocation;
  - resolve template, SR, optional network, and optional host placement;
  - clone the template and apply Crabbox lease labels;
  - generate cloud-init user-data and metadata;
  - build and attach a config-drive disk;
  - start the VM;
  - wait for guest metrics to report a non-loopback IPv4;
  - wait for SSH readiness and hand back a normal direct SSH lease target.
- Implements resolve/list/touch/release/cleanup behavior for XCP-ng leases so existing direct-lease command paths work with the new provider.
- Cleans up failed provisioning attempts by deleting the config drive and cloned VM when bootstrapping cannot complete.
- Removes stored testbox keys on release and keeps cleanup restricted to Crabbox-managed XCP-ng resources.

### Xen API client and VM/disk handling

- Adds a substantial XAPI client in `internal/providers/xcpng/client.go` covering login/logout, session handling, pool redirect handling, VM lookup/clone/start/shutdown/delete flows, storage and VBD/VDI operations, guest metrics polling, and label updates.
- Uses durable VM identifiers and safer ownership checks so destructive cleanup paths stay scoped to the VM/config-drive resources Crabbox created.
- Redacts secrets from XML-RPC faults, upload/import paths, and transport errors to avoid leaking session IDs, passwords, or tokens in user-visible failures.
- Hardens cleanup behavior around shutdown/power-state transitions, config-drive detach ordering, clone rollback, and disk ownership.

### Cloud-init and config-drive generation

- Adds XCP-ng-specific cloud-init/config-drive generation in `internal/providers/xcpng/cloudinit.go`.
- Generates a per-lease SSH-enabled bootstrap payload that:
  - creates the Crabbox SSH user and authorized key;
  - configures the SSH port contract Crabbox expects;
  - prepares the remote work root and cache directories;
  - installs required bootstrap packages with bounded apt retry behavior;
  - marks readiness for the normal Linux bootstrap flow.
- Builds the config drive as a FAT16 image in-process rather than depending on external tooling.
- Rejects oversized config-drive payloads cleanly instead of panicking.

### CLI, config, and command-surface integration

- Extends the global config model with `XCPNgConfig` and corresponding environment/config loading in `internal/cli/config.go` and related config command tests.
- Adds non-secret XCP-ng CLI flags for API URL, username, template, SR, network, host, user, work root, and insecure TLS.
- Intentionally does **not** add an argv password flag, so XCP-ng credentials stay in config files, env vars, or secret stores rather than shell history/process lists.
- Integrates XCP-ng into provider help, command docs, config diagnostics, provider backend routing, and server-type derivation.
- Validates unsupported provider/target combinations in doctor/provisioning paths while still allowing existing XCP-ng lease operations to proceed when a local config target has drifted into an unsupported provisioning mode.

### Doctor support

- Adds non-mutating XCP-ng doctor support that validates required config, opens an XAPI session, resolves configured placement, and lists Crabbox-managed inventory.
- Reports `mutation=false` and surfaces configuration/placement failures in a way suitable for local setup checks and CI gating.

### Guarded live smoke tooling

- Adds `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh` as a guarded smoke helper for real XCP-ng environments.
- Supports:
  - read-only mode that runs doctor and writes redacted evidence only;
  - explicit mutating mode gated behind `CRABBOX_XCP_NG_LIVE_MUTATE=1`, which creates a kept lease, runs a minimal no-sync command, and stops that lease.
- Redacts both HTTP and HTTPS pool URLs plus secret-like fields from retained evidence.
- Preserves caller-supplied environment variables over local env-file defaults so CI or explicit operator overrides win.

## Hardening and Follow-Up Fixes Included Here

In addition to the base provider work, this branch includes a long tail of correctness and safety fixes discovered during review and mutation-driven follow-up, including:

- XAPI pool-master redirect handling;
- UUID vs `OpaqueRef` handling and durable VM identity selection;
- stricter ownership checks for VM disks and config-drive cleanup;
- safer cleanup ordering around detach/shutdown/destroy flows;
- guest-IP filtering and timeout behavior improvements;
- template/config validation and doctor placement checks;
- logout/session cleanup correctness;
- cloud-init quoting, SSH contract, and package bootstrap resiliency;
- live-smoke cleanup and evidence redaction fixes.

## Documentation

- Adds a full provider guide at `docs/providers/xcp-ng.md` covering setup, template requirements, configuration, doctor behavior, lifecycle expectations, and live-smoke usage.
- Updates command docs and feature/configuration docs so XCP-ng appears as a first-class supported provider throughout the docs surface.
- Updates source-map/provider index references so the new package is discoverable.

## Tests

This PR adds broad regression coverage across:

- provider registration and provider help surfaces;
- config parsing and config-command output;
- backend routing and provider target validation;
- XCP-ng lifecycle behavior and cleanup edge cases;
- Xen API client request/response/error handling and redaction;
- cloud-init/config-drive generation and bounds checking;
- live-smoke script behavior and redaction rules.

The largest new test surfaces are:

- `internal/providers/xcpng/backend_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/client_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/cloudinit_test.go`
- `internal/providers/xcpng/provider_test.go`
- `scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`

## Related

- Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217
- Follows up on https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/217#issuecomment-4637370969

## Verification

- `node --test scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.test.js`
- `bash -n scripts/xcpng-live-smoke.sh`
- `GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache-pr222-final go test ./internal/cli -run '^TestDoctorRejectsUnsupportedProviderTarget$|^TestLoadLeaseTargetConfigAllowsExistingLeaseDespiteUnsupportedProvisioningTarget$|^TestLoadLeaseTargetConfigRejectsUnsupportedProviderTarget$|^TestLoadLeaseTargetConfigAllowsAWSMacOSWithoutProvisioningHost$' -count=1`
- `GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache-pr222-final go test ./internal/providers/xcpng -run 'Test(CloseRetainsSessionWhenLogoutFailsSoItCanRetry|HaltedPowerStateFaultRequiresActualHaltedState|CloudInitPayload|BuildConfigDriveImage|ConfigDriveLabels|DoctorUsesReadOnlyPlacementAndInventory|DoctorValidatesOptionalPlacementReadOnly|DoctorReportsPlacementFailureWithoutInventory|DoctorFailsConfigurationWhenTemplateMissing|AcquireLifecycleCallOrderAndTarget|AcquireCleansUpVMAndConfigDriveOnGuestIPFailure|AcquireRetriesFreshLeaseWhenGuestIPNeverAppears|WaitForGuestIPv4ClassifiesGuestMetricsNoIPAsBootstrapTimeout|ResolveRejectsExistingNonCrabboxVM|ResolveByAliasReturnsGuestIPLookupErrorWhenHostMissing|ListResolveTouchReleaseUseOnlyCrabboxMetadata|ReleaseRemovesStoredKey|CleanupIsMetadataAndExpiryGated|CleanupDryRunDoesNotDelete|ValidationSplitsConnectionFromProvisioningPlacement|XMLRPCRequestEncodingKeepsCredentialOutOfURL|XAPIStatusEnvelopeUnwrapsValueAndFailure|XAPIStatusEnvelopeRedactsSecrets|XMLValueToStringAcceptsBareCharacterData)$' -count=1`
- `GOCACHE=/tmp/crabbox-go-cache-pr222-final scripts/check-docs.sh`
- `git diff --check`

## Notes

- PR head is `799d6bb` on `feat/xcp-ng-provider`.
- Live smoke remains opt-in and still requires local XCP-ng credentials and pool access.
- During isolated worker execution, some broader listener-backed Go slices were sandbox-blocked; the integrated writable checkout reran the relevant focused regression slices above before push.
target.author: coygeek
